evidence_statements.faithful_clinical_statement: Rosacea PIL (Apr 2022) Scan to share What are the aims of this leaflet? This leaflet has been written to help you understand more about rosacea. It explains what rosacea is, what causes it, what can be done about it, and where you can find more information about it. What is rosacea? Rosacea is a common skin condition, mostly affecting the face. It predominantly affects fair-skinned individuals but may appear in any skin type. Rosacea can start at any age from childhood onwards, but most commonly appears in young adults. It is more common in women however, when affecting men, it may be more severe. It is a long-term condition, can persist for a long time and fluctuates from mild to severe. Rosacea mostly affects the cheeks, forehead, chin, and nose. More rarely, it may affect the neck and chest. It appears as persistent redness caused by dilated blood vessels, small bumps, and pus-filled
